source: Hofstede Insights — 6-D Model of National Culture

PDI.label: Power Distance
PDI.description: Italy scores 50 — a medium level. In Northern Italy hierarchy is less felt and there is more equality, while Southern Italy tends to be more hierarchical.

IDV.label: Individualism
IDV.description: Italy is an individualist culture (76). Individual achievement is valued, especially in Northern regions. Southern Italy shows more collectivist traits with stronger family ties.

MAS.label: Masculinity
MAS.description: Italy scores 70 — a masculine society. Competition, success and achievement are important drivers. Children are taught that winning is important.

UAI.label: Uncertainty Avoidance
UAI.description: Italy scores 75 — high uncertainty avoidance. Italians prefer clear structures, rules and planning. Bureaucracy and formality serve to reduce ambiguity.

LTO.label: Long Term Orientation
LTO.description: Italy scores 61 — a pragmatic culture. Italians believe truth depends on situation, context and time. They show ability to adapt traditions to changed conditions.

IVR.label: Indulgence
IVR.description: Italy scores 30 — a restrained culture. Societies with low IVR tend to cynicism and pessimism, control gratification of desires, and feel that social norms restrain their actions.

north.description: Northern Italy (Lombardia, Piemonte, Veneto, Emilia-Romagna, etc.)

tendencies.PDI: Lower — flatter hierarchies, more egalitarian workplaces
tendencies.MAS: Similar or slightly higher — competitive business culture
tendencies.IVR: Similar — restrained but with higher work-life balance focus

tendencies.PDI: Medium — balanced between north and south
tendencies.IDV: Medium-high — mix of individual and community orientation
tendencies.MAS: Medium — quality of life emphasis (especially Toscana)

south.description: Southern Italy (Campania, Puglia, Calabria, Sicilia, Sardegna, etc.)

tendencies.IDV: Lower — stronger family and community bonds
tendencies.UAI: Higher — stronger need for certainty and stability
tendencies.IVR: Lower — more restrained, stronger social norms
